blocks:
Sūrat Tabbat

And it is named Sūrat Abī Lahab. It is Meccan, and it consists of five verses by consensus (بالإِجماع)* بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ*** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) تَبَّتْ يَدا أَبِي لَهَبٍ ** (1) Perish the hands of Abū Lahab, Meaning: they lost and perished, for al-tabāb (التّبات) is a loss (خسران) that leads to perdition (الهلاك). It was said that by his hands (يَدَيْهِ), his soul (نَفْسُهُ) was intended, like His saying: وَلا تُلْقُوا بِأَيْدِيكُمْ ** (2) and do not cast [yourselves] into destruction with your own hands. And it was said, rather, what is intended is his worldly life (دُنْيَاهُ) and his Hereafter (آخِرَتُهُ). ** وَتَبَ ** and perish he! (This is) a declaration after a declaration, or an imprecation against him after an imprecation.

(٢) ما أَغْنى عَنْهُ مالُهُ وَما كَسَبَ ** (2) His wealth and what he earned did not avail him. when the destruction (التّبات) descended upon him. It is said that he died of the plague (العدسة) a few days after the Battle of Badr, and he was left for three [days/nights] until he putrefied. Then some of the black slaves (السودان) were hired, and they buried him. (٣) سَيَصْلى ناراً ذاتَ لَهَبٍ. ** (3) He shall enter a fire full of flame, (٤) وَامْرَأَتُهُ ** (4) and his wife, and she is Umm Jamil, the sister of Abu Sufyan. حَمَّالَةَ الْحَطَبِ ** the carrier of the firewood, It is said that [it] means the firewood of Hell (حطب جهنّم), for she used to carry sins (الأوزار) by showing enmity towards the Messenger and inciting her husband to harm him. And it is said that rather, what is meant by it is the bundle of thorns (الشوك) and caltrops (الحسك) that she used to carry and spread at night on the path of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. And it was recited with the nasb (النّصب) [accusative case] as an insult (الشتم).

(٥) فِي جِيدِها حَبْلٌ مِنْ مَسَدٍ ** (5) In her neck is a rope of palm fibre. Meaning, from what was twisted (مُسِدَ), that is, spun (فُتِلَ), meaning, from fire (نَار).

The Qummi [said regarding] تَبَّتْ يَدا أَبِي لَهَبٍ ** (1) Perish the hands of Abu Lahab, and perish he! He said: Meaning, they were ruined (خَسِرَتْ) when he gathered with Quraysh in Dar al-Nadwah (دَار النَّدْوَة) and pledged allegiance to them to kill Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And he possessed great wealth, so Allah said:

**ما أَغْنى عَنْهُ مالُهُ وَما كَسَبَ سَيَصْلى ناراً ذاتَ لَهَبٍ ** (2) Neither his wealth nor what He has earned will avail him. (3) He will soon enter a fire of intense flame, upon him, so it will burn him.

وَامْرَأَتُهُ حَمَّالَةَ الْحَطَبِ ** (4) and his wife, the carrier of the firewood, He said: She was Umm Jamil, daughter of Sakhr, and she used to slander (تَنِمُّ) the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and convey his sayings (أَحَادِيثَهُ) to the disbelievers (الْكُفَّار).

حَمَّالَةَ الْحَطَبِ ** (4) and his wife, the carrier of the firewood, Meaning, she gathered firewood (اِحْتَطَبَتْ) against the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. فِي جِيدِها (in her neck), meaning, in her neck (عُنُقِهَا) is a rope مِنْ مَسَدٍ ** (of palm fibre), meaning, of fire (نَار). He said: The name of Abu Lahab was 'Abd Manaf, but Allah gave him a kunya (كُنَّاهُ) because Manaf (مَنَاف) was an idol (صَنَم) they worshipped.

In al-Majma' (الْمَجْمَع) regarding the Almighty's saying: وَأَنْذِرْ عَشِيرَتَكَ الْأَقْرَبِينَ. (26:214) And warn your nearest kinsfolk. Narrated from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: When this verse was revealed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم ascended al-Safa (الصَّفَا) and called out: "O morning calamity (يَا صَبَاحَاه)!" Quraysh gathered around him and asked: "What is wrong with you?" He said: "Tell me, if I informed you that the enemy would attack you in the morning or the evening, would you believe me?" They said: "Yes." He said: "Then I am a warner to you before a severe punishment." Abu Lahab said: "Perish you (تَبّاً لَكَ)! Is this why you gathered us all?" So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed تَبَّتْ يَدا أَبِي لَهَبٍ وَتَبَ (Perish the hands of Abu Lahab, and perish he!)—the entire Surah.

In Qurb al-Isnad (قُرْب الْإِسْنَاد), narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) in a hadith concerning the signs (آيَات) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He said: "And among those [signs] is that Umm Jamil, the wife of Abu Lahab, came to him when Surat Tabbat (سُورَة تَبَّتْ) was revealed. Abu Bakr ibn Abi Quhafah was with the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and he said: 'O Messenger of Allah, this is Umm Jamil, enraged (مُحْفَظَة), meaning, angered (مُغْضَبَة), seeking you, and she has a stone with her, intending to throw it at you.' He [the Prophet] said: 'She will not see me.' So she said to Abu Bakr: 'Where is your companion?' He replied: 'Where Allah willed.' She said: 'I have come for him, and if I saw him, I would surely throw [the stone] at him, for he has satirized me. By al-Lat (اللَّات) and al-Uzza (الْعُزَّى), I am indeed a poetess (شَاعِرَة).' Abu Bakr then asked: 'O Messenger of Allah, why did she not see you?' He replied: 'No, Allah placed a veil (حِجَاباً) between me and her.'"

In Thawab al-A'mal (ثَوَاب الْأَعْمَال) and al-Majma' (الْمَجْمَع), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: "When you recite تَبَّتْ يَدا أَبِي لَهَبٍ وَتَبَ (Perish the hands of Abu Lahab, and perish he!), then supplicate against Abu Lahab, for he was one of those who denied the Prophet and what came from Allah the Almighty."
